bluesky.text: Hormuz Risk Clock, 11 Mar 2026: transit flow remains below 10% of pre-conflict levels; bypass capacity stays at 3.5-5.5 mb/d vs ~20 mb/d normal flow. IEA approved a 400m-barrel emergency release. Priors: reopening 25%, effective closure 55%, wider escalation 20%.

reddit.title: As of: 2026-03-11T18:35:57Z
reddit.body:
# Hormuz Risk Clock Snapshot

As of: 2026-03-11T18:35:57Z

## States
- **transit_flow**: 4/4 (flat-high) — IEA says export volumes of crude oil and refined products are currently at less than 10% of pre-conflict levels
- **attack_tempo**: 4/4 (flat-high) — MARAD 2026-001A says vessels should keep clear if possible
- **insurance_availability**: 4/4 (flat-high) — cover scarce, repriced, or withdrawn
- **navigation_integrity**: 3/4 (flat-high) — persistent GNSS/GPS and AIS disruption
- **bypass_capacity**: 3/4 (flat-high) — IEA cites 3.5-5.5 mb/d of bypass capacity versus about 20 mb/d normal Hormuz flow
- **asia_buffer_stress**: 1/4 (flat) — IEA members approved a 400 million barrel emergency stock release to cushion market disruptions

## Branch priors
- **reopening**: 25%
- **effective_closure**: 55%
- **wider_escalation**: 20%
